title: Telegram message bubble collector (6s idle, lifecycle-aware)
current_state: Telegram bot dispatches every message to process_input independently. User sending 'Hey/Supppp/Hey' in 5 seconds gets three separate replies. L2 distillation runs on N-message thresholds, fragmenting natural conversational units. There is no notion of a 'conversational bubble'.
structural_problem: Pi treats messages as atomic. Conversations are not. Humans send incomplete thoughts, then finish them; rapid bursts are one intent. Without a bubble abstraction, every downstream concern (recall, distillation, response cost) compounds the fragmentation.
target_state: agent/bubble.py exports BubbleCollector. Telegram bot enqueues messages; collector waits for IDLE_MS (default 6000 — Ash override from 12s) of silence per chat_id, then emits a single Bubble{messages: [...], started_at, ended_at, has_media, reply_targets: [...]} to a consumer queue. Bubble force-flushes on lifecycle events (/exit, /clear, any media message). process_input consumes one bubble at a time. Three rapid 'Hey's produce ONE response. L2 distillation triggers per-bubble, not per-message.
files_affected: tools/tools_telegram.py — handlers enqueue, separate consumer thread dispatches bubbles to process_input
memory/pipeline.py — bubble close becomes the L2 distillation trigger
design_notes: BubbleCollector(idle_ms=6000, max_bubble_ms=120000, max_messages=20) — bubble closes on (a) idle timeout, (b) hard upper bound, (c) message cap, (d) lifecycle event (/exit, /clear)
Concurrency: lock per chat_id; timer in daemon thread; consumer queue shared
Bubble joiner: 'Hey\nSupppp\nHey' (newline-separated) so the LLM sees them as three thoughts
Media bubble: any media message closes the bubble immediately (don't make user wait for vision)
Lifecycle force-flush: BubbleCollector.flush(chat_id) called by /exit and /clear handlers BEFORE any other work; ensures partial bubble is not lost
Reply_to_message preservation: collector stashes each msg.reply_to_message.text in bubble.reply_targets — needed by T-123
Edge case 1: message arrives during flush — new message starts a fresh bubble (no loss)
Edge case 2: /exit during open bubble — force flush, dispatch, THEN run exit_steps
Edge case 3: /clear during open bubble — force flush, dispatch under OLD session_id, THEN rotate session_id
Terminal mode unaffected: bubble layer ONLY wraps Telegram handlers
success_criteria: 3 messages within 5s produce exactly 1 bubble (verified: idle=6s, gaps < 6s keep extending)
20+ messages without idle still flush at message cap
Single bubble produces single L2 distillation entry (not N)
/exit during open bubble: response is sent BEFORE goodbye message
/clear during open bubble: response is sent under old session_id, then new session begins
Terminal mode behaviour unchanged (verify with test)
risk_notes: Latency risk: 12s wait could feel slow to fast typers. Mitigation: tunable env var PI_BUBBLE_IDLE_MS; show 'typing...' indicator while bubble open. Concurrency risk: race between flush timer and new-message arrival. Mitigation: per-chat_id lock + atomic test-and-flush.
migration_plan: 2. Unit tests with controlled clock (freezegun or manual time injection)
3. Insert collector between Telegram handler and process_input — single consumer thread
4. Move L2 distillation trigger from message-count to bubble-close event
5. Integration test: simulate Telegram messages, assert bubble shape
